operation: First, a 10 mm x 10 mm x 1 mm rectangular box is created on the XY workplane. Next, a workplane is established on the top (positive Z direction) face of the base box, a centered 6 mm x 6 mm rectangle is sketched, then extruded 1 mm upward to form the middle tier. A new workplane is then created on the top face of the middle rectangular tier, a centered circle with 2 mm radius is sketched, extruded 1 mm upward to form the top cylindrical feature, before all features are combined into a single unified solid.
geometry: This is a symmetric three-tiered stacked solid aligned along a shared vertical central axis. The lowest tier is a square prism, the middle tier is a smaller square prism positioned concentrically on the upper face of the base tier, and the topmost tier is a right circular cylinder centered on the upper face of the middle square tier, with each tier having identical height.
function: This stepped concentric component is intended for use as a compact standoff, spacer, or small mounting adapter, providing graded height offset support and a central cylindrical interface for alignment, attachment, or positioning in light assembly applications.
